evals.prompt: Download https://martinfowler.com/articles/gen-ai-patterns/ and save it as markdown in ./outputs/. Keep the article title as the H1 and include the source URL near the top.
evals.expected_output: One markdown file in ./outputs/ containing the article converted into readable markdown with a title, source URL, retrieval date, and preserved headings/links.

evals.prompt: Fetch this blog post and save it as oil-water-moment.md: https://www.infoq.com/articles/oil-water-moment-ai-architecture/. I want proper markdown with headers, lists, and links kept intact.
evals.expected_output: A file named oil-water-moment.md with article metadata at the top and clean markdown content below it.

evals.prompt: Convert these two URLs into markdown files inside ./articles/: https://huyenchip.com/2024/07/25/genai-platform.html and https://labs.lamatic.ai/p/gen-ai-architecture. Save one .md file per URL and keep the source URL inside each file.
evals.expected_output: Two markdown files in ./articles/, one per URL, each including title, source URL, retrieval date, and converted body content.
